title: cymbidium eburneum var. parishii
intro: Cymbidium eburneum var. parishii, commonly known as the Parish's Cymbidium, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Native to the Himalayas, this plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been used in traditional medicine for various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The therapeutic actions of Cymbidium eburneum var. parishii include antioxidant, anti-diabetic, and anti-hypertensive effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids.
